items.title: Russian Hackers Exploit Zimbra Zero-Day Against US, Ukraine Targets
items.summary: A state-sponsored threat group, dubbed "Laundry Bear," sends "half-click" phishing emails that require a victim only to open or preview the message.

items.title: New Dolphin X malware uses AI to rank high-value targets
items.summary: A new Dolphin X remote access trojan claims to use an AI-powered profiling feature to score and rank infected users, helping cybercriminals identify which victims should be targeted first. [...]

items.title: Australian energy provider Origin says data breach exposes client data
items.summary: Origin Energy has confirmed that an unauthorized party accessed and subsequently leaked customer data online, exposing sensitive personally identifiable information (PII), among others. [...]

items.title: Fake Claude app promoted by Bing ads pushes SectopRAT malware
items.summary: A malvertising campaign on the Bing search service is pushing a fake Claude desktop app installer hosted on a legitimate Claude.ai domain to deliver the SectopRAT malware. [...]

items.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
items.summary: A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client. The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organization's entire email…

items.summary: CISA is warning that the Russian state-sponsored hacking group Laundry Bear, also known as Void Blizzard, is targeting organizations using Zimbra Collaboration email servers by combining phishing attacks with the…

items.title: Hackers abuse Notepad++ plugins to stealthily install malware
items.summary: Ukraine's CERT has uncovered attacks distributing an archive containing the legitimate Notepad++ application and a malicious utility called LunchPoke disguised as a plugin to establish persistence. [...]

items.title: OpenAI Fixes ChatGPT Agent Flaw That Could Let Attackers Forge an AI Insider

items.title: Is Patching Dead? Vulnerability Management in the Post-Mythos Era
items.summary: You cannot out-patch a machine that writes a working exploit from a vulnerability description in twenty hours. Stop trying to optimize a game you cannot win. The post Is Patching Dead? Vulnerability Management in the…

items.title: Chick-fil-A Accounts Get Fried in Credential Stuffing Attack
items.summary: Threat actors used credentials obtained from other companies to hack into Chick-fil-A One accounts. The post Chick-fil-A Accounts Get Fried in Credential Stuffing Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New RefluXFS Linux flaw lets attackers gain root privileges
items.summary: A nine-year-old race condition vulnerability in the Linux kernel's XFS filesystem, tracked as CVE-2026-64600, allows local attackers to overwrite protected files and gain root privileges. [...]

items.title: Agentic AI Challenges Progress in Confidential Computing
items.summary: Core issues that slowed down adoption of secure data vaults are being resolved by technology, but artificial intelligence poses new ones. Experts have some answers.

items.title: Upbound Group Says Data Breach Led to $13 Million in Fraudulent Contract Losses
items.summary: Hackers recently obtained non-sensitive customer information and other documents from the company. The post Upbound Group Says Data Breach Led to $13 Million in Fraudulent Contract Losses appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New msaRAT malware uses Chrome, Edge browsers to route C2 traffic
items.summary: The Chaos ransomware gang is using a new backdoor dubbed msaRAT that hides command-and-control (C2) communication by routing it through the Chrome or Edge browsers. [...]
